epigraph.text:
The plans of the diligent lead to profit as surely 
as haste leads to poverty.

content.text:
Many equations require two steps to solve. The key is knowing which 
step to do first: undo addition or subtraction before undoing 
multiplication or division.
When solving two-step equations, we reverse the order of operations:

Think about getting dressed: you put on socks, then shoes. To get 
undressed, you remove shoes first, then socks—the reverse order!

content.title: Solving Two-Step Equations
content.content:
1. Undo addition or subtraction (isolate the term with the variable)

2. Undo multiplication or division (isolate the variable)

3. Check your answer in the original equation

content.title: Two steps: add then divide
content.solution:
$$
3x + 5 &= 20 && \text{Original equation} \\
3x + 5 - 5 &= 20 - 5 && \text{Subtract 5 from both sides} \\
3x &= 15 && \text{Simplify} \\
\frac{3x}{3} &= \frac{15}{3} && \text{Divide both sides by 3} \\
x &= 5 && \text{Simplify}
$$

**Check:** $3(5) + 5 = 15 + 5 = 20$

content.content:
Solving equations teaches patience. As Proverbs 21:5 reminds us, 
careful planning leads to success. Each step builds on the previous 
one, bringing us closer to the truth we seek.

content.text:
When the variable term is divided by a number, multiply after undoing 
addition or subtraction.

content.title: Division in the equation
content.solution:
$$
\frac{x}{4} + 3 &= 7 \\
\frac{x}{4} + 3 - 3 &= 7 - 3 && \text{Subtract 3 from both sides} \\
\frac{x}{4} &= 4 \\
4 \cdot \frac{x}{4} &= 4 \cdot 4 && \text{Multiply both sides by 4} \\
x &= 16
$$

**Check:** $\dfrac{16}{4} + 3 = 4 + 3 = 7$

content.title: Another division example
content.solution:
$$
\frac{n}{-3} - 5 &= 1 \\
\frac{n}{-3} &= 6 && \text{Add 5 to both sides} \\
n &= -18 && \text{Multiply both sides by __MATH_BLOCK_0__}
$$

**Check:** $\dfrac{-18}{-3} - 5 = 6 - 5 = 1$

content.text: When the coefficient is negative, be extra careful with signs.

content.title: Negative coefficient
content.solution:
$$
-2x + 9 &= 3 \\
-2x + 9 - 9 &= 3 - 9 && \text{Subtract 9 from both sides} \\
-2x &= -6 \\
\frac{-2x}{-2} &= \frac{-6}{-2} && \text{Divide both sides by __MATH_BLOCK_0__} \\
x &= 3
$$

**Check:** $-2(3) + 9 = -6 + 9 = 3$

content.title: Scripture
content.content:
Even when signs are negative or numbers seem to go in the "wrong" 
direction, the solution exists and can be found. Romans 8:28 promises 
that "in all things God works for the good." Even negative numbers 
have their place in the beautiful order of mathematics.

marginNotes.text:
There is a time for everything, and 
a season for every activity under the heavens.
Ask and it will be given to you.

marginNotes.author: Carl Sagan
marginNotes.text:
Somewhere, something incredible is waiting 
to be known.

marginNotes.author: Ada Lovelace
marginNotes.text:
The more I study, the more insatiable do I 
feel my genius for it to be.
